prompt:
You are an Amazon SP-API listing specialist and an expert at writing search-engine-optimized (SEO) Amazon listings. For the product below, write an item_name of AT MOST 75 characters and a title_differentiation of AT MOST 125 characters. Always return both, each within its own limit. === PRODUCT CONTEXT === SKU: DOY-STAY-DRY-KIT-DRY-SACK-BUNDLE Product Type: SURVIVAL_KIT Brand (from catalog): ASR Outdoor Existing Title: Stay Dry Kit Dry Sack Camping Description: Stay dry in the great outdoors. This kit has everything you need to stay dry and warm in case it starts to rain, hail, or snow while camping.

Kit includes a reusable Mylar waterproof and windproof survival blanket that reflects 90% of your body heat back to you. This can be used not only as a blanket, but as shelter or ground cover.

Also included is gear to start a fire to help stay warm and dry, as well as cook food. We have incorporated a 12 pack of slow burning tinder sticks that are non-toxic and water resistant. Compact and lightweight, these are great for starting fires and keeping them going. Each stick takes at least 5 minutes to burn through the exterior.

In order to start the fire, the 3-in-1 Water Resistant Plastic Container included acts as a match holder, fire starter, and mirror. The mirror is ideal for signalling, and the fire starter is an easy to use flint on the bottom. Finally, we included a waterproof dry sack to store all of the necessary camping supplies and keep them dry. Dry sack has a volume of 20L.

The bright orange color of the bag, blanket, and container ensure they are easy to spot and are ideal for emergency signalling. Don't forget this kit on your next adventure to handle everything mother nature throws at you. Search Terms: waterproof gear bag keep items dry out of water camping gear accessories backpack storage bag gift outdoors camping nessecity river boat kayaking safety stow bag Features: - Includes a waterproof dry sack for carrying items - Includes a waterproof fire starter/mirror/container - Includes a waterproof survival blanket - Includes water-resistant 12 pack of tinder sticks - Great value! === ITEM_NAME (title, <= 75 chars) === Write a concise, keyword-rich, SEO-optimized item_name — a shopper-facing title, not a part number. Derive these tokens, then assemble the title from them in order: ${brand} ${pack_size}-${pack_size_unit} ${size} ${color} ${name} - brand: use the catalog brand above. - pack_size + pack_size_unit: parse from the existing title (e.g. "4-pk", or a dimension like 13" -> pack_size "13", pack_size_unit "in"). Omit if absent. - size, color, name: parse from the existing title (name = what the product IS). Drop any token you cannot determine; never emit the SKU, MPN, or a part number. Lead with the brand; condense to AT MOST 75 characters. Amazon schema guidance: Provide a title for the item that may be customer facing === TITLE_DIFFERENTIATION (item highlight, 100–125 chars) === Write a compact, comma-separated list of the product's strongest, most search-relevant features/benefits — NOT a full sentence, NOT the brand, and NOT a repeat of the item_name (e.g. "Set of 6, Dishwasher Safe, 8oz, BPA-Free, Stackable"). Use the available space: aim for 100–125 characters, leading with the highest-value keywords. Never pad with filler or repeat a point. Capitalize the first letter of every word (e.g. "Natural Wood Grain", but keep acronyms like "US" fully uppercase). Do not use special characters — spell out "&" as "and" and "=" as "is" (e.g. "Yellow Is Real", "Works On US And International Currency"); only hyphen, double quote, and apostrophe are allowed. Amazon schema guidance: Provide product features or benefit driven phrases, not a full sentence. Information will appear only when the item name is under 75 characters. Do not repeat information already in the item name. === RETURN === Return ONLY a valid JSON object, no markdown fences, in this exact shape: {"item_name": "<= 75 char title", "tokens": {"brand": "", "pack_size": "", "pack_size_unit": "", "size": "", "color": "", "name": ""}, "title_differentiation": "<= 125 char benefit phrase"}
